title: GPU Memory Leak in Training Loop
description:
## The Situation

Your PyTorch training job has been crashing every run. The model architecture hasn't changed, the dataset is the same — but after a few epochs the process dies with:

```
RuntimeError: CUDA out of memory. Tried to allocate 2.00 GiB
(GPU 0; 15.00 GiB total capacity; 13.50 GiB already allocated;
512.00 MiB free; 14.20 GiB reserved in total by PyTorch)
```

## Your Task

1. Connect to the server (you already have a terminal)
2. Examine `train.py` — find the bug causing memory to leak
3. Fix it so the training job runs all 5 epochs successfully
4. Click **Check Solution** to verify your fix

## Useful Commands

```bash
python3 train.py          # Run the training script
nvidia-smi                # Check GPU memory usage
cat train.py              # View the code
nano train.py             # Edit the code
```
hints: GPU memory grows when PyTorch tensors that hold computation graphs are kept alive longer than needed. Look for any data structure that accumulates tensors across iterations.

In the training loop, `loss` is a tensor with a full computation graph attached. If you store it in a list, the graph (and all the memory it references) stays allocated. What method converts a tensor to a plain Python number?

PyTorch tensors have a method that extracts the scalar value as a plain Python float, breaking the link to the computation graph. Look it up in the PyTorch docs — it's one of the most commonly used tensor methods in training loops.
